item.title: Newark’s detention center requires real accountability
item.description: The federal government has an obligation to ensure that immigrant detainees are not subjected to cruel conditions.

item.title: Did Trump win the late-night comedy battle?
item.description: Did President Donald Trump kill late-night television shows, or did comedy podcasts simply beat them at their own game? In this episode of "Make It Make Sense," James Hohmann, Carine Hajjar and Damir Marusic explore the massive cultural shift in comedy during the Trump era.

item.title: Mexico’s Claudia Sheinbaum has found her scapegoat
item.description: Anti-U.S. rhetoric helps her avoid accountability ahead of elections.

item.title: ‘Mine’s self-driving’
item.description: Mark Wood cartoon on self-driving cars.

item.title: What Tuesday told MAGA and the Democratic establishment
item.description: Trump lost one, Democrats dodged some: Here's what the primary results revealed.

item.title: Trump wanted to abandon this ‘highest duty.’ The WHO said no.
item.description: The World Health Organization has deftly handled attempts by countries to leave.

item.title: A great reverse migration is shifting the balance of power in the U.S.
item.description: Republicans in Southern states are gerrymandering the return of Black voters.

item.title: Why $1 million doesn’t feel rich for Gen Z
item.description:
The average American household is now worth more than $1 million. Yet Gen Z is increasingly pessimistic about its economic future. Why does it feel like so many people are falling behind? Is homeownership out of reach? And how can young people build wealth in an economy that seems both richer and more uncertain than ever?
Megan McArdle talks with Ed Elson, co-host of Prof G Markets, about wealth inequality, housing affordability, social media and the frustrations shaping younger generations' outlook.

item.title: Lessons from Trump’s National Guard experiment
item.description: The deployment reduced property crimes, but smarter policing was also crucial.

item.title: Inside the lives of the last Jews in Berlin
item.description: A new novel offers a disturbing account of the stark choices faced by Jews in Nazi Germany.

item.title: Do these words make you sound smarter? The bias is spreading.
item.description: English speakers love the Romance vocabulary. AI noticed.

item.title: California is at a ‘breaking point.’ Steve Hilton says he knows why.
item.description: The former Fox News host sees a path to becoming the first Republican elected governor in 20 years.

item.title: Why people stopped having kids
item.description: The fertility crisis is also a religion crisis.

item.title: An astonishing interstate trade war is coming to the Supreme Court
item.description: States should not attempt economic regulation beyond their borders.

item.title: The Enhanced Games was a manifestation of wellness culture gone awry
item.description: Athletes pumping their bodies with chemicals to maximize performance sets a terrible example for everyone else.

item.title: The shell game
item.description: Michael Ramirez cartoon on Iran war negotiations.
